name: Roast Pesto Chicken
recipeInstructions:
Serves 4
Fresh basil sprigs
Pat the chicken dry. Slide your hand between the chicken skin and meat over the breast and legs to form pockets. Reserve 1 tablespoon pesto for gravy; spread the remaining pesto under the skin and over the breast and leg meat of the chicken, in the cavity of the chicken, and over the outer skin. Tie the legs together to hold their shape. Tuck the wings under the body. Place the chicken in a large roasting pan.
Preheat the oven to 450 degrees F. Roast the chicken for 15 minutes. Reduce the oven temperature to 375 degrees F and roast until the juices run clear when the chicken is pierced in the thickest part of the thigh, basting occasionally with pan juices, about 1 hour 15 minutes. Transfer the chicken to a platter.
Pour the pan juices into a glass measuring cup; degrease. Add wine to the roasting pan and bring to boil, scraping up any browned bits. Add the wine mixture and any drippings from the platter to the pan juices. Add enough broth to measure 1 cup. Transfer to a heavy small saucepan.
Combine 2 tablespoons of broth and flour in a bowl; stir until smooth. Add to the saucepan. Bring to boil, whisking constantly. Boil until reduced to sauce consistency, stirring often, about 5 minutes. Mix in cream and the reserved 1 tablespoon pesto. Season with salt and pepper. Garnish the chicken with basil sprigs. Serve with gravy.
